root_text.sn46.11:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci pāṇā cattāro iriyāpathe kappenti—
root_text.sn46.11:1.2: kālena gamanaṁ, kālena ṭhānaṁ, kālena nisajjaṁ, kālena seyyaṁ, sabbe te pathaviṁ nissāya pathaviyaṁ patiṭṭhāya evamete cattāro iriyāpathe kappenti;
root_text.sn46.11:1.3: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkaroti.
root_text.sn46.11:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn46.11:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ …pe…
root_text.sn46.11:2.3: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn46.11:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya satta bojjhaṅge bhāveti, satta bojjhaṅge bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn46.11:1.1: “Mendicants, living creatures engage in the four postures:
translation_text.sn46.11:1.2: sometimes walking, sometimes standing, sometimes sitting, sometimes lying down. They do so depending on the earth and grounded on the earth.
translation_text.sn46.11:1.3: In the same way, a mendicant develops and cultivates the seven awakening factors depending on and grounded on ethics.
translation_text.sn46.11:2.1: And how does a mendicant develop the seven awakening factors depending on and grounded on ethics?
translation_text.sn46.11:2.2: It’s when a mendicant develops the awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion,
translation_text.sn46.11:2.3: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn46.11:2.4: That’s how a mendicant develops and cultivates the seven awakening factors depending on and grounded on ethics.”
